SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION OF GERALDINE ARMENDARIZ IN OPPOSITION OF DEFENDANTS MOTION TO REOPEN DISCOVERY (TRANSACTION ID # 57734665) FILED BY PLAINTIFF MEDINA, LETICIA MEDINA, YVETTE REYES

DISCOVERY, 302, AS TO DEFENDANT LINH LIEU TRAN'S MOTION TO REOPEN DISCOVERY, THE MOTION GRANTED IN PART; DENIED IN PART. DEFENDANT MAY SERVE ADDITIONAL THIRD PARTY SUBPOENAS CONCERNING THE SUBSEQUENT ACCIDENTS AND PLAINTIFFS' MEDICAL TREATMENT. HOWEVER, ANY OTHER DISCOVERY BEYOND THOSE SUBPOENAS, INCLUDING FURTHER DEPOSITIONS, IS DENIED AT THIS JUNCTURE. AFTER RECEIPT OF THE THIRD PARTY SUBPOENAS, DEFENDANT MAY MAKE A SUBSEQUENT MOTION FOR MORE DISCOVERY, BUT MUST ARTICULATE ADDITIONAL GOOD CAUSE AND THE OTHER FACTORS REQUIRED BY REGAN V. LANET (1987) 197 CAL.APP.3D 353. COURT TO SIGN PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED BY DEFENSE COUNSEL. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID MCDONALD, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reopen Discovery
Off Calendar Aug-31-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. AS to DEFENDANT LINH LIEU TRAN'S Motion To Reopen Discovery, the motion granted in part; denied in part. Defendant may serve additional third party subpoenas concerning the subsequent accidents and Plaintiffs' medical treatment. However, any other discovery beyond those subpoenas, including further depositions, is denied at this juncture. After receipt of the third party subpoenas, Defendant may make a subsequent motion for more discovery, but must articulate additional good cause and the other factors required by Regan v. Lanet (1987) 197 Cal.App.3d 353. Court to sign proposed order submitted by defense counsel. Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/JPT)
